error_signature: property.split is not a function or its return value is not iterable
title: Gmail node attachment error due to incorrect field name
symptom: When trying to send an attachment via the Gmail node, an error occurs stating 'property.split is not a function or its return value is not iterable'. This prevents the email from being sent with the intended attachment.
applies_when: Using Gmail node to send an email
Attempting to attach a file from Google Drive node
not_this_if: Attachment field name is correctly set to a binary property
root_cause: The Gmail node requires the attachment field name to correspond to a binary property from the previous node. If the field name is incorrectly set to a literal string instead of the actual binary property name, it leads to the mentioned error.
fix_steps: Locate the Attachment Field Name option
Set the field name to 'data' (or the correct binary property name)
Save the workflow and try sending the email again
agent_instruction: Set the Attachment Field Name in the Gmail node to 'data' to resolve the error.
